tasks.subject: US-X-001: Feed loads in under 2 seconds [frontend-designer]
tasks.description:
Source: .spec/prd/subscriptions-redesign/tasks/epic-5-infrastructure/US-X-001.md

================================================================================
TASK: US-X-001 - Feed loads in under 2 seconds
================================================================================

TASK_TYPE: FEATURE
STATUS: To Do
TDD_PHASE: RED
CURRENT_AC: AC-1
PRIORITY: P0
EFFORT: S
TYPE: DEV
ITERATION: 1

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
BACKGROUND
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Problem:** Feed may take too long to render on initial load, blocking user engagement.

**Why it matters:** Users abandon apps that take more than 2 seconds to show content.

**Current state:** Feed screen exists but lacks performance optimization (FlatList tuning, skeleton states).

**Desired state:** Feed renders visible content within 2 seconds of screen mount.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (TDD Beads)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

AC-1: Feed renders within 2 seconds
  GIVEN: User opens feed
  WHEN: Screen loads
  THEN: Feed visible in under 2 seconds

  TDD_STATE: [ ] RED  [ ] VERIFY_RED  [ ] GREEN  [ ] VERIFY_GREEN  [ ] REFACTOR
  TEST_FILE: components/subscriptions/__tests__/SubscriptionFeedScreen.perf.test.tsx
  TEST_FUNCTION: feedRendersWithinTwoSeconds

Quality Criteria:
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Lint passes with zero errors
- [ ] Type check passes
- [ ] RED evidence in Linear comments

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
READING LIST
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. components/subscriptions/SubscriptionFeedScreen.tsx
   - Lines: ALL
   - Focus: Current FlatList setup, data loading pattern

2. components/subscriptions/FeedCard.tsx
   - Lines: ALL
   - Focus: Card render cost, memoization

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GUARDRAILS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

WRITE-ALLOWED (explicit file list):
- components/subscriptions/SubscriptionFeedScreen.tsx (MODIFY) - FlatList performance props
- components/subscriptions/FeedSkeleton.tsx (NEW) - skeleton loading state
- components/subscriptions/__tests__/SubscriptionFeedScreen.perf.test.tsx (NEW)

WRITE-PROHIBITED:
- convex/** - backend changes separate
- app/** - routing changes separate

MUST:
- [ ] Use FlatList with initialNumToRender, maxToRenderPerBatch, windowSize
- [ ] Render skeleton states immediately while data loads
- [ ] One test per acceptance criterion

MUST NOT:
- [ ] Block feed rendering waiting for all images
- [ ] Add features beyond AC requirements

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CODE PATTERN (Reference)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

```typescript
<FlatList
  data={findings}
  keyExtractor={(item) => item.url}
  renderItem={({ item }) => <FeedCard item={item} />}
  initialNumToRender={10}
  maxToRenderPerBatch={10}
  windowSize={5}
  ListEmptyComponent={isLoading ? <FeedSkeleton count={5} /> : <EmptyState />}
/>
```

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
AGENT ASSIGNMENT
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Implementation Agent**: frontend-designer
**Rationale**: React Native FlatList performance optimization

**Review Agent**: react-native-ui-reviewer
**Rationale**: Validates RN performance patterns

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EVIDENCE GATES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Gate 1: All Tests Pass
  Command: pnpm vitest run
  Expected: Exit 0

Gate 2: Type Check
  Command: pnpm tsc --noEmit
  Expected: Exit 0

Gate 3: Lint
  Command: pnpm eslint --fix
  Expected: Exit 0

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DEPENDENCIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Depends On:
- Epic 2 (Multimedia Card Stream) - cards must exist

Blocks:
- None

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
NOTES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- Focus on perceived performance (skeletons) as much as actual load time
- Performance profiling required for verification

================================================================================

tasks.subject: US-X-003: Skeleton states while images load [frontend-designer]
tasks.description:
Source: .spec/prd/subscriptions-redesign/tasks/epic-5-infrastructure/US-X-003.md

================================================================================
TASK: US-X-003 - Skeleton states while images load
================================================================================

TASK_TYPE: FEATURE
STATUS: To Do
TDD_PHASE: RED
CURRENT_AC: AC-1
PRIORITY: P0
EFFORT: S
TYPE: DEV
ITERATION: 1

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
BACKGROUND

**Problem:** Cards show blank space or nothing while images load, creating a jarring experience.

**Why it matters:** Progressive loading with skeletons makes the app feel fast even when data is still arriving.

**Current state:** Cards render but may not show skeleton/placeholder during image load.

**Desired state:** Skeleton shows immediately, then image fades in when loaded.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

```typescript
<FastImage
  source={{
    uri: thumbnailUrl,
    priority: FastImage.priority.normal,
    cache: FastImage.cacheControl.web
  }}
  style={styles.thumbnail}
  resizeMode={FastImage.resizeMode.cover}
  defaultSource={require('@/assets/images/placeholder.png')}
/>
```

================================================================================
TASK: US-X-004 - Offscreen image cancellation and memory stability
================================================================================

TASK_TYPE: FEATURE
STATUS: To Do
TDD_PHASE: RED
CURRENT_AC: AC-1
PRIORITY: P0
EFFORT: S
TYPE: DEV
ITERATION: 1

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
BACKGROUND

**Problem:** Fast scrolling may load images for cards that are already offscreen, wasting bandwidth and memory.

**Why it matters:** Unnecessary image loads cause memory pressure and wasted data usage.

**Current state:** Images may continue loading even after scrolling past their cards.

**Desired state:** Offscreen images are cancelled and memory remains stable during fast scroll.

tasks.subject: US-X-007: Offline banner appears on network loss [frontend-designer]
tasks.description:
Source: .spec/prd/subscriptions-redesign/tasks/epic-5-infrastructure/US-X-007.md

================================================================================
TASK: US-X-007 - Offline banner appears on network loss
================================================================================

TASK_TYPE: FEATURE
STATUS: To Do
TDD_PHASE: RED
CURRENT_AC: AC-1
PRIORITY: P1
EFFORT: S
TYPE: DEV
ITERATION: 1

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
BACKGROUND
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Problem:** Users don't know they're offline and may think the app is broken.

**Why it matters:** Clear offline indication sets expectations and prevents confusion.

**Current state:** No offline indicator exists.

**Desired state:** "Offline" banner appears at top of feed when network is lost.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (TDD Beads)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

AC-1: Offline banner appears
  GIVEN: User goes offline
  WHEN: Network lost
  THEN: "Offline" banner appears at top

  TDD_STATE: [ ] RED  [ ] VERIFY_RED  [ ] GREEN  [ ] VERIFY_GREEN  [ ] REFACTOR
  TEST_FILE: components/subscriptions/__tests__/OfflineBanner.test.tsx
  TEST_FUNCTION: showsOfflineBannerWhenNetworkLost

Quality Criteria:
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Lint passes with zero errors
- [ ] Type check passes
- [ ] RED evidence in Linear comments

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
READING LIST
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. components/subscriptions/SubscriptionFeedScreen.tsx
   - Lines: ALL
   - Focus: Where to place banner

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GUARDRAILS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

WRITE-ALLOWED (explicit file list):
- components/subscriptions/OfflineBanner.tsx (NEW) - offline indicator
- hooks/useNetworkStatus.ts (NEW) - network detection hook
- components/subscriptions/SubscriptionFeedScreen.tsx (MODIFY) - integrate banner
- components/subscriptions/__tests__/OfflineBanner.test.tsx (NEW)

WRITE-PROHIBITED:
- convex/** - backend caching handled by Convex

MUST:
- [ ] Use NetInfo for network detection
- [ ] testID="offline-banner" on the banner

MUST NOT:
- [ ] Block UI when offline

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CODE PATTERN (Reference)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

```typescript
import NetInfo from '@react-native-community/netinfo'

export function useNetworkStatus() {
  const [isOnline, setIsOnline] = useState(true)
  useEffect(() => {
    const unsubscribe = NetInfo.addEventListener(state => {
      setIsOnline(state.isConnected ?? true)
    })
    return unsubscribe
  }, [])
  return { isOnline, isOffline: !isOnline }
}
```

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
AGENT ASSIGNMENT
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Implementation Agent**: frontend-designer
**Rationale**: React Native network status UI

**Review Agent**: react-native-ui-reviewer
**Rationale**: Validates offline UX patterns

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EVIDENCE GATES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Gate 1: All Tests Pass
  Command: pnpm vitest run
  Expected: Exit 0

Gate 2: Type Check
  Command: pnpm tsc --noEmit
  Expected: Exit 0

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DEPENDENCIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Depends On:
- Epic 2 (Multimedia Card Stream) - feed screen must exist
- Epic 4 (Feedback) - feedback needs offline queueing

- None

================================================================================

tasks.subject: US-X-009: Feedback queued when offline [frontend-designer]
tasks.description:
Source: .spec/prd/subscriptions-redesign/tasks/epic-5-infrastructure/US-X-009.md

================================================================================
TASK: US-X-009 - Feedback queued when offline
================================================================================

TASK_TYPE: FEATURE
STATUS: To Do
TDD_PHASE: RED
CURRENT_AC: AC-1
PRIORITY: P1
EFFORT: S
TYPE: DEV
ITERATION: 1

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
BACKGROUND
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Problem:** User feedback is lost when submitted offline.

**Why it matters:** Users expect their actions to persist; losing feedback erodes trust.

**Current state:** Feedback submission fails silently when offline.

**Desired state:** Feedback is queued locally with a visible indicator when offline.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (TDD Beads)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

AC-1: Feedback queued with indicator
  GIVEN: User provides feedback offline
  WHEN: Tap thumbs up
  THEN: Feedback queued, indicator shown

  TDD_STATE: [ ] RED  [ ] VERIFY_RED  [ ] GREEN  [ ] VERIFY_GREEN  [ ] REFACTOR
  TEST_FILE: hooks/__tests__/useOfflineQueue.test.ts
  TEST_FUNCTION: queuesFeedbackWhenOffline

Quality Criteria:
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Lint passes with zero errors
- [ ] Type check passes
- [ ] RED evidence in Linear comments

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
READING LIST
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. components/subscriptions/FeedbackButtons.tsx
   - Lines: ALL
   - Focus: Current feedback submission pattern

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GUARDRAILS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

WRITE-ALLOWED (explicit file list):
- hooks/useOfflineQueue.ts (NEW) - feedback queue hook
- components/subscriptions/FeedbackButtons.tsx (MODIFY) - offline handling
- hooks/__tests__/useOfflineQueue.test.ts (NEW)

WRITE-PROHIBITED:
- convex/** - backend changes separate

MUST:
- [ ] Use AsyncStorage for queue persistence
- [ ] Show queue indicator to user

MUST NOT:
- [ ] Lose user feedback
- [ ] Fail silently

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CODE PATTERN (Reference)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

```typescript
const FEEDBACK_QUEUE_KEY = 'offline_feedback_queue'

interface QueuedFeedback {
  findingId: string
  sentiment: 'positive' | 'negative'
  timestamp: number
}

export function useOfflineQueue() {
  const { isOnline } = useNetworkStatus()

  const queueFeedback = async (feedback: QueuedFeedback) => {
    const queue = await getQueue()
    queue.push(feedback)
    await AsyncStorage.setItem(FEEDBACK_QUEUE_KEY, JSON.stringify(queue))
  }

  return { queueFeedback, getQueue }
}
```

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
AGENT ASSIGNMENT
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Implementation Agent**: frontend-designer
**Rationale**: React Native offline queue pattern

**Review Agent**: react-native-ui-reviewer
**Rationale**: Validates offline data patterns

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EVIDENCE GATES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Gate 1: All Tests Pass
  Command: pnpm vitest run
  Expected: Exit 0

Gate 2: Type Check
  Command: pnpm tsc --noEmit
  Expected: Exit 0

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DEPENDENCIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- Epic 4 (Feedback) - feedback buttons must exist

tasks.subject: US-X-010: Queued feedback submits on reconnect [frontend-designer]
tasks.description:
Source: .spec/prd/subscriptions-redesign/tasks/epic-5-infrastructure/US-X-010.md

================================================================================
TASK: US-X-010 - Queued feedback submits on reconnect
================================================================================

TASK_TYPE: FEATURE
STATUS: To Do
TDD_PHASE: RED
CURRENT_AC: AC-1
PRIORITY: P1
EFFORT: S
TYPE: DEV
ITERATION: 1

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
BACKGROUND
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Problem:** Queued offline feedback stays in local storage and never reaches the server.

**Why it matters:** Feedback data is useless if it never syncs to the backend.

**Current state:** Offline queue exists (US-X-009) but no flush mechanism.

**Desired state:** Queued feedback automatically submits when network is restored.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (TDD Beads)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

AC-1: Queued feedback submitted on reconnect
  GIVEN: User goes back online
  WHEN: Network restored
  THEN: Queued feedback submitted automatically

  TDD_STATE: [ ] RED  [ ] VERIFY_RED  [ ] GREEN  [ ] VERIFY_GREEN  [ ] REFACTOR
  TEST_FILE: hooks/__tests__/useOfflineQueue.test.ts
  TEST_FUNCTION: flushesQueueOnReconnect

Quality Criteria:
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Lint passes with zero errors
- [ ] Type check passes
- [ ] RED evidence in Linear comments

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
READING LIST
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. hooks/useOfflineQueue.ts
   - Lines: ALL
   - Focus: Queue structure, flush logic

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GUARDRAILS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

WRITE-ALLOWED (explicit file list):
- hooks/useOfflineQueue.ts (MODIFY) - add flush on reconnect
- hooks/__tests__/useOfflineQueue.test.ts (MODIFY)

WRITE-PROHIBITED:
- convex/** - backend changes separate

MUST:
- [ ] Flush queue automatically when isOnline changes to true
- [ ] Clear queue after successful submission

MUST NOT:
- [ ] Lose queued feedback on flush failure
- [ ] Block UI during flush

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
AGENT ASSIGNMENT
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Implementation Agent**: frontend-designer
**Rationale**: React Native offline sync pattern

**Review Agent**: react-native-ui-reviewer
**Rationale**: Validates sync patterns

  Expected: Exit 0

tasks.subject: US-X-011: Cache expires after 7 days [frontend-designer]
tasks.description:
Source: .spec/prd/subscriptions-redesign/tasks/epic-5-infrastructure/US-X-011.md

================================================================================
TASK: US-X-011 - Cache expires after 7 days
================================================================================

TASK_TYPE: FEATURE
STATUS: To Do
TDD_PHASE: RED
CURRENT_AC: AC-1
PRIORITY: P1
EFFORT: S
TYPE: DEV
ITERATION: 1

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
BACKGROUND
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Problem:** Cached content may become stale and misleading if never expired.

**Why it matters:** Users shouldn't see month-old content without knowing it's outdated.

**Current state:** No cache expiration mechanism exists.

**Desired state:** Cache older than 7 days is cleared; user sees empty state if offline with expired cache.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (TDD Beads)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

AC-1: Expired cache cleared
  GIVEN: Cache > 7 days old
  WHEN: User opens feed
  THEN: Cache cleared, fresh content loaded (empty state if offline)

  TDD_STATE: [ ] RED  [ ] VERIFY_RED  [ ] GREEN  [ ] VERIFY_GREEN  [ ] REFACTOR
  TEST_FILE: components/subscriptions/__tests__/SubscriptionFeedScreen.offline.test.tsx
  TEST_FUNCTION: clearsExpiredCacheAfterSevenDays

Quality Criteria:
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Lint passes with zero errors
- [ ] Type check passes
- [ ] RED evidence in Linear comments

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
READING LIST
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. components/subscriptions/SubscriptionFeedScreen.tsx
   - Lines: ALL
   - Focus: Cache loading pattern

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GUARDRAILS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

WRITE-ALLOWED (explicit file list):
- components/subscriptions/SubscriptionFeedScreen.tsx (MODIFY) - cache expiry
- components/subscriptions/__tests__/SubscriptionFeedScreen.offline.test.tsx (MODIFY)

WRITE-PROHIBITED:
- convex/** - backend changes separate

MUST:
- [ ] 7-day cache expiration (CACHE_MAX_AGE = 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000)
- [ ] Clear stale cache on load

MUST NOT:
- [ ] Never expire cache
- [ ] Show stale content without indication

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
